summary: Plan 13 is implementation-ready after its two live blockers close. The repaired plan replaces the insecure implicit browser grant with public-client authorization code plus S256 PKCE, validates an exact issuer-bound authorization/token endpoint pair, hardens URL parser differentials and browser callback handling, validates Cognito access tokens with an explicit client_id profile, and makes CSP, logging, redaction, shared-provider regressions, repository gates, and tracker handoff executable.

review_basis.head: 41039f85a922a9ada297048042998d3256a3ab02
review_basis.baseline: Live Plan 13, Plans 00/01/10/12, the runtime-readiness specification, public release guarantee, current WebSettings/app/discovery/JWKS/OIDC/Entra/routes/CLI/LoginPage/CSP code and tests, current Filigree DAG, and current dirty-worktree state were inspected. Loomweave was refreshed at the live HEAD. Primary RFC, AWS Cognito, and PyJWT documentation supplied the OAuth, token-claim, PKCE, and expiry-requirement authority.

warnings.detail: Plan 13 remains pending and unassigned. It is correctly blocked by Plan 01 elspeth-b9e8b5d24b and shared signed-tier/trust-boundary/Wardline baseline elspeth-8166b310e7. This review did not claim or transition it.

Cognito necessarily returns the short-lived PKCE-bound code in the callback query. Plan 13 disables Uvicorn raw access logs and adds no-referrer/no-cache handling; Plan 12 proves the disposable ALB has access logging disabled. A production ALB that enables access logs needs an approved retention/access policy because frontend cleanup cannot redact an upstream request log.

The accepted access token continues through ELSPETH's existing auth-store localStorage session policy. Plan 13 does not silently redesign product-wide session persistence; it prevents persistence of authorization codes, PKCE verifiers, ID/refresh tokens, raw token responses, and acceptance evidence.

The browser token exchange must pass the exact dynamic CSP and Cognito CORS behavior in Plan 10's real hosted-UI lane. Failure is NO-GO; workers may not fall back to implicit tokens, widen connect-src, or introduce a browser client secret.

The shared worktree contains earlier plan-review changes and unrelated concurrent work. The review preserved them. Plan 13 execution requires a clean isolated worktree and exact changed-versus-staged path equality before commit.

resolved_findings.resolution: Replaced the implicit response_type=token architecture with a public-client authorization-code flow using transaction-specific S256 PKCE, immediate callback cleanup, and no client secret; Plans 10/12 and the specification now require code-only live acceptance.

Specified a paired authorization/token endpoint validator with exact normalized origins, independent allowlist validation, explicit port semantics, ASCII A-label policy, browser/parser differential rejection, unsafe-IP controls, and honest initial-destination-only scope.

Added issuer-bound browser and JWKS discovery, explicit endpoint-pair configuration, generic JSON collection wiring, model-time allowlist validation, exact public config shape, and raw-input-redacted settings failures.

Changed the browser transaction to store only state, verifier, and timestamp; set an exact five-minute lifetime; scrub before async work; require fresh backend auth config; remove URI bearer-token paths; and cover duplicate/error/mismatch/StrictMode replay.

Added a 64 KiB streamed token-response cap, 16 KiB access-token cap, no-redirect/no-credentials/no-referrer/no-store fetch posture, static errors, and no persistence of ID/refresh tokens or raw responses.

Removed the conflicting static CSP meta policy and required an equivalent dynamic response-header CSP whose connect-src adds only the validated token origin; added CSP, Referrer-Policy, callback cache, and built-index tests.

Made callback-query logging explicit: Uvicorn raw access logs are disabled and tested, the runbook documents upstream retention, and live acceptance proves its disposable ALB access logs are disabled.

Defined the Cognito client_id token profile as exact client ID plus token_use=access, required exp/iat/iss/sub/client_id/token_use, issuer/signature/key-ID checks, and Cognito RS256 independent of the untrusted header; default aud mode gains an explicit exp requirement.

Pinned audience_claim as keyword-only after cache arguments, added create_app wiring proof and Entra shared-validator regressions, and corrected public guarantee/trust-boundary metadata from ID-token/nonce claims to the actual access-token/PKCE boundary.

Added full Python/frontend/build/contracts/signed-tier/trust-boundary/Wardline gates, expanded elspeth-8166b310e7 to own the known honesty failure, and made that shared baseline a direct Plan 13 dependency.

Added isolated-worktree execution, atomic Filigree start/comment/close, exact changed-path equals staged-path proof, pre-commit and clean-post-commit requirements, and aligned typed tracker fields without claiming the issue during review.
Strengthened Plan 12 rollback-baseline source sentinels and Cognito app-client preflight so a partial Plan 13 implementation or implicit-enabled client cannot qualify.

review_history.summary: Initial review found a production implicit-token grant, incomplete origin canonicalization, optional expiry, unbound discovery, incomplete callback cleanup, raw-input reflection, insufficient shared-provider/gate coverage, and unsafe tracker/staging handoff.

First repair pass closed the OAuth/JWT/origin core but exposed runtime CSP blocking, mutable sessionStorage authority, unbounded token responses, callback-query logging, and incomplete signed/staging guarantees.

verification: Three independent reviewers returned APPROVED after repairs.
Loomweave was refreshed at the live release/0.7.1 HEAD and current endpoint/JWKS/settings/app/frontend call surfaces were rechecked.
Plan 10 and Plan 12 Bash fences concatenate and pass bash -n; Markdown fences are balanced.

git diff --check passes for the repaired plan/spec set; stale implicit-enabled preflight, oidc_state rollback sentinel, and memory-only access-token claims are absent except where explicitly described as removed or forbidden behavior.

Filigree Plan 13 fields and dependency edges match the repaired plan; the issue remains pending, unassigned, and blocked. The shared baseline issue now owns signed tier, trust-boundary honesty, and Wardline restoration.
This review did not implement Plan 13, start/claim/close its issue, commit, push, merge, tag, deploy, or stage unrelated files.
